routineText:
09:00 — Wake up & go to Work
09:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
13:00
13:00 — Black Coffee + 1 tsp of sugar

-----------------------
| 14:00 — 🌅 Breakfast
| — whole wheat bread 35g
| — 6 whites & 3 yolks of beldi eggs
| — eggs fried with 5g of olive oil
| — 2 garlic cloves cooked
| — 5g of raw olive oil
| — mint tea + 1 tsp of sugar
| — cumin & salt
| — oat 20g + warm water (↓ from 35g, to offset restored oil)
| — 1 scoop of protein powder with water
| — 1 apple (small, ~150g)
-----------------------

14:00 — Return to Work
14:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
18:00
18:00 — Out from work

-----------------------
| 18:00 — ☀️ Lunch
| — kefta 90/10 200g raw
| — kefta cooked with 1 onion (~50g)
| — kefta cooked with 5g of olive oil
| — brown rice 20g raw (kept at 20g minimum)
| — 300g legume sauté with 5g of olive oil (40% carrot, 40% courgette, 20% onion)
| — cumin & salt
-----------------------

18:00 — Black Coffee
18:00
      — (4h) Rest or Billard & Water 1L
21:00
21:00 — Back Home

-----------------------
| 21:00 — 🌙 Dinner
| — 2 scoops of protein powder (↑ from 1.5)
| — 250g raib (homemade, from Jaouda Sans Lactose)
| — 1 kiwi
| — 5g Almonds
| — 5g Walnuts
| — 5g Cashews
| — 5g Pistachios
| — 5g Pumpkin Seeds
-----------------------

01:00 — Sleep

adjustments.reason: Part of the 2-raib : 1-perly rotation across the 10-day plan. A full 250g serving (not an equal-weight swap) is what actually moves calcium — raib is less calcium-dense than Perly per gram, so 85g would have been a step backward. Calcium rises from 20% to 31%; carbs/fat both nudge up but stay in the good range.

adjustments.from: Kefta lunch with both 5g cooking oils removed and rice trimmed to 8g
adjustments.to: Both cooking oils restored (5g each), rice kept at a 20g floor, oat cut 35g→20g to offset
adjustments.reason: Cooking oil and legume-sauté oil are not on the table for removal, and rice never goes below 20g when rice is on the menu. Oat is the lever instead. Carbs land back in the good range (128g, 98.6%), but oat's fat content is too low (0.069g/g) to offset 10g of restored oil fat — fat settles at ~110% (warn, not bad). Kcal 103.5% and protein 105.2% stay good.

verdict.text_html: Rest day, warn — 103% of calories; fat runs ~110% since cooking oil stays and oat can't offset it

coverage.name: Calories
coverage.note: Re-tuned under the new directional rules (protein floor, carb/fat ceilings)

coverage.name: Protein
coverage.note: See fullTable for the protein-source breakdown

coverage.name: Carbs
coverage.note: Trimmed to sit under the new 100% ceiling

coverage.name: Fat
coverage.note: Trimmed to sit under the new 100% ceiling

coverage.name: Fiber
coverage.note: Oat, legume sauté, apple, and kiwi contribute, but no potato/banana in the rotation

coverage.name: Gas / bloat risk
coverage.note: Bread once, cumin at breakfast and lunch, onion only cooked

coverage.name: Protocol adherence
coverage.note: Bread once ✓, dinner well before 22:00 ✓, cumin at breakfast/lunch ✓

coverage.name: Selenium
coverage.note: Strong across all three rotation versions

good: Protein 105%, carbs 99%, fat 110% — oat alone can't offset the restored cooking oil, so fat runs a bit hot
Vitamin B12 at 241% and Selenium at 162% — kefta's strongest points
Vitamin A (140%) and Vitamin C (129%) both comfortably covered
Biotin (250%) and B5 (128%) round out a solid micronutrient day
bad: Calcium (20%), Vitamin D (10%), Chromium (9%), Vitamin E (23%) — the usual structural gaps, no dairy/fish/avocado
Fat is the highest of the three versions (110%) — kefta's own fat plus the restored cooking oil
Iron (69%) and Magnesium (39%) both mid-pack — better than chicken/thon but not strong
Fiber still short at ~72% — no potato/banana in the rotation
loggedItems: 6 egg whites + 3 egg yolks (beldi), fried with 5g olive oil
Kefta 90/10 200g raw, cooked with 1 onion (~50g) and 5g olive oil
300g légumes sautés (carrot 120g, courgette 120g, onion 60g) with 5g olive oil
5g Almonds + 5g Walnuts + 5g Cashews + 5g Pistachios + 5g Pumpkin Seeds

fullTable.note: Kefta 200g + 1.5 whey scoops + eggs — comfortably past target
